messages.author: El-samiyel
messages.text: So i've been working on a new t-deck+ firmware. These builds are experimental and may contain bugs, but they help accelerate development through community testing and feedback. https://tdeck.ubnzeek.com Very rough and rusty video here: https://youtu.be/zrLql42LDCw?si=7Gx1FwqCxopjxEcz MeshUltra is a custom UI firmware for meshcore. (edited)

messages.author: El-samiyel
messages.text: Hi SM, I’ve just finished making a few updates. Patreon is no longer required, you can now purchase the software directly here: https://tdeck.ubnzeek.com/ The site is still a work in progress, but it’s fully functional. There’s a web flasher available, and you can purchase multiple licences if needed. Documentation on features will be added soon, but for now it’s best to try it yourself as the firmware is evolving daily. (edited)
messages.content_html: Hi SM, I’ve just finished making a few updates. Patreon is no longer required, you can now purchase the software directly here: https://tdeck.ubnzeek.com/ The site is still a work in progress, but it’s fully functional. There’s a web flasher available, and you can purchase multiple licences if needed. Documentation on features will be added soon, but for now it’s best to try it yourself as the firmware is evolving daily. (edited)
messages.reply_html:
SM-
Do you sell the base version on Pateon for $40? and then $15 every month for the updates? This is according to what I am reading there. Also, is the licence per device only? (edited)

messages.author: bluebrolly.
messages.text: Hey credit where credit is due. I have had too many projects bum me out where I am just like... is this even worth it and at the verge of failure. I know there is MeshOS out there and a few other alternatives to the screen ui stuff made by different teams but its amazing all the ui elements running at the same time and the speed looks pretty decent.
messages.content_html: Hey credit where credit is due. I have had too many projects bum me out where I am just like... is this even worth it and at the verge of failure. I know there is MeshOS out there and a few other alternatives to the screen ui stuff made by different teams but its amazing all the ui elements running at the same time and the speed looks pretty decent.

messages.reply_html:
El-samiyel
Haha too complimentary man, but thanks. I just find it addictive trying to squeeze the most of an esp whilst keeping things usable. Have to say, im pretty surprised at this myself especially with the companion app and UI running in tandem.

messages.author: El-samiyel
messages.text: Hi @TheBoyd78 unfortunately not, I was kind of put off by a few Reddit comments, so I decided to model the launch this way. It doesn’t work for everyone, but for those who do support, they’ll get full support from me in return. Framing it this way helps attract the right people who understand that its a WIP firmware that's built to give a nice UI whilst pushing the ESP32-S3 to its limits.
messages.content_html: Hi @TheBoyd78 unfortunately not, I was kind of put off by a few Reddit comments, so I decided to model the launch this way. It doesn’t work for everyone, but for those who do support, they’ll get full support from me in return. Framing it this way helps attract the right people who understand that its a WIP firmware that's built to give a nice UI whilst pushing the ESP32-S3 to its limits.

messages.author: El-samiyel
messages.text: Thanks for the feedback. What are you using a T-Deck or the plus? For entering channels on the device itself, this issue hasn't been reported so will look into it (checked just now, cant reproduce this). As for the companion, only what has been demoed on the video is solid atm. For maps SDroot: maps/0-9 is where you should place the folders (If something else - let me know what in particular is not working on the maps screen).
messages.content_html: Thanks for the feedback. What are you using a T-Deck or the plus? For entering channels on the device itself, this issue hasn't been reported so will look into it (checked just now, cant reproduce this). As for the companion, only what has been demoed on the video is solid atm. For maps SDroot: maps/0-9 is where you should place the folders (If something else - let me know what in particular is not working on the maps screen).

messages.author: El-samiyel
messages.text: Fix for the companion app not allowing channels to be added has been uploaded to the portal. Still need more info on any other bugs that may have cropped up. Summary of changes: Companion App: When you add a channel in the app, it is replicated on the T-Deck. Two blank icons are now the repeater manager and recent heard adverts. Messages blank panel now shows total stats (storage space, total msgs etc.) Tested all other companion app features and they seem to work. More detail here: https://tdeck.ubnzeek.com/project-updates
messages.content_html:
Fix for the companion app not allowing channels to be added has been uploaded to the portal. Still need more info on any other bugs that may have cropped up. Summary of changes:
  • Companion App: When you add a channel in the app, it is replicated on the T-Deck.
  • Two blank icons are now the repeater manager and recent heard adverts.
  • Messages blank panel now shows total stats (storage space, total msgs etc.)
  • Tested all other companion app features and they seem to work.
More detail here: https://tdeck.ubnzeek.com/project-updates
